task_completion.description: Open the NetCDF dataset and select the 'prate' variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a data viewer (like Panoply) with 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' open and the 'prate' (Surface Precipitation Rate) variable selected, or a script explicitly loading this file and variable.

task_completion.description: Generate a 12-month annual cycle line plot for the Amazon Basin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 1D line plot window showing precipitation rate over time (12 months). The plot settings or title should indicate it is for lat ≈ -3, lon ≈ 300. The plot curve should depict a year-round humid tropical rainfall pattern.

task_completion.description: Export the Amazon Basin plot to the specified filename and directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing 'amazon_annual_precip.png' being saved to or existing in '~/Documents/PrecipRegimes/'.

task_completion.description: Generate a 12-month annual cycle line plot for the Eastern Mediterranean.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 1D line plot window showing precipitation rate over time (12 months). The plot settings or title should indicate it is for lat ≈ 38, lon ≈ 20. The plot curve should depict a pronounced summer drought (a significant dip in the middle months).

task_completion.description: Export the Eastern Mediterranean plot to the specified filename and directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing 'mediterranean_annual_precip.png' being saved to or existing in '~/Documents/PrecipRegimes/'.
A text editor open showing 'regime_comparison_report.txt' in '~/Documents/PrecipRegimes/' with filled-in text comparing the Amazon and Mediterranean precipitation regimes.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a genuine plotting tool (e.g., Panoply) or wrote a script to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Panoply GUI (creating 1D plots, adjusting lat/lon arrays) or a Python/R script being written and executed, rather than downloading pre-made images from the internet.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the exact requested coordinates for the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: The plot creation interface (e.g., Panoply Array 1 or Array 2 settings) or script must explicitly show lat -3 / lon 300 for the Amazon plot and lat 38 / lon 20 for the Mediterranean plot.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually filled out or generated the comparison report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent editing 'regime_comparison_report.txt' in a text editor, rather than copying a pre-existing completed file from an unauthorized location.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target dataset is ~/PanoplyData/prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc and the variable to plot is 'prate' (Surface Precipitation Rate). The Amazon Basin coordinates are lat -3, lon 300, which should show a year-round humid tropical rainfall pattern. The Eastern Mediterranean coordinates are lat 38, lon 20, which should show a pronounced summer drought. The expected output files are amazon_annual_precip.png, mediterranean_annual_precip.png, and regime_comparison_report.txt located in ~/Documents/PrecipRegimes/.
